doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_clear_flags (mpfr/src/exceptions.c L144-L150). Three-line C body: `__gmpfr_flags = 0` -- wipe the entire global flag register. C returns void.

The TS port follows the immutable flag-family contract: takes `mask: bigint` describing the pre-clear flag state, applies the wipe-all operation, returns the new flag state as `bigint`. The output is ALWAYS `0n` regardless of input mask -- that's the contract.

In-library wiring goes through clearFlags() (with no argument; defaults to MPFR_FLAGS_ALL) from src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- the input domain is the 6-bit mask space; the output is constant `0n`. Coverage focuses on verifying the constant return (any input -> 0).
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Return type: C returns void (mutates global state); TS returns `bigint` (the new flag state = always 0n).

  2. Parameter list: C takes void; TS takes `mask: bigint` (pre-clear state). The mask is largely informational -- the output is always 0n -- but it's the same wire form the sister flag-clearing ports use, so the input is preserved for consistency. The reference port returns 0n unconditionally; a correct production port should do the same.

  3. Mechanism: clearFlags() in src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts (no arg -> defaults to MPFR_FLAGS_ALL -> wipes everything). Inline: simply `return 0n`.
refs: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L144-L150 -- the C reference body.
src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts -- shipped TS flag register; clearFlags() is the canonical entry.
eval/functions/mpfr_clear_divby0/spec.json -- sibling clear-bit template (single bit; clear_flags clears all).
